title: quercus ilex subvar. cordifolia
intro: Quercus ilex subvar. cordifolia, also known as the cork oak, is a subspecies of the holm oak tree that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The bark of the Quercus ilex subvar. cordifolia has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Quercus ilex subvar. cordifolia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
